methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Lila Sciences is a Series A biotech company founded by Geoffrey von Maltzahn and Molly Gibson, having raised $200M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies across Biotech & Health—3 public-market references and 8 private companies at seed through Series B stages, including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B), and Neko Health ($1.8B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $200M raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, yielding $400M. Peer-median anchoring across the 6 most comparable same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies produces a $1.3B median valuation, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via EV/Revenue multiples cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

The triangulated valuation range is $340M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median implied valuation of $850M. The wide range reflects heterogeneity in peer stage composition and recent frontier-biotech market volatility. Key uncertainties include the target's revenue trajectory (not disclosed), the comparability of peers across varying therapeutic modalities, and the sustainability of current biotech multiples in a higher-rate environment.
